note: design↔live element correspondence for PRD-P8's runtime-state FOOT of the first-run local-model card. Kept SEPARATE from anchors.json (which maps the whole `gate` screen) because these anchors are per-state and their labels would collide with the gate's `card.local.*` set. The card HEADER (icon/title/meta/body) is identical in every state and is already scored by anchors.json; only `card` + `card.meta` are repeated here, `card.meta` because it carries the D5 divergence and `card` because it gives each per-state report its box/type context.
usage: ?state= is REQUIRED on the design side — without it the mock renders all five catalog columns and every `.fr-gcard` selector below would resolve to the first one. FILTER `elements` by the `state` field when building an extraction spec: a few selectors (`.fr-dep .acts .gbtn--pri`, `.fr-dep .watch`) legitimately match in more than one state, so an unfiltered spec would cross-align labels. Labels are state-prefixed and therefore globally unique, so one anchors file drives all five reports.

designStateNotes.detected: the mock has NO `?state=detected`. Load `?state=not-installed` and CLICK the `Get Ollama ↗` button — the vendored jsx flips its local `rt` to "found", which is the only way its `.ok` line renders.
designStateNotes.fail: `?state=fail` exists in the vendored mock but is the DROPPED "Download failed" state (PRD-P8 D1 deletes the red terminal branch). It has no live analog and is deliberately unmapped.

blindSpots: The `.watch` leading dot is a `::before` pseudo-element in the live CSS (onboarding.css `.fr-dep .watch::before`), and `getComputedStyle(el)` per-element extraction cannot address a pseudo-element. See the `not-installed.watch.dot` anchor.

The mock draws the watch dot ONLY in state ① (a `<span class="d">`); its state ④ watch line has no dot at all. The live `::before` applies to BOTH watch lines, so live ④ shows a dot the mock does not. The extractor cannot see either dot, so this delta will never appear in a report — it is recorded here instead. PRD-P8 §5 specifies the dot for ① only, so live ④ is a (cosmetic, unscored) divergence.

expectDivergence.fontSize: HARNESS ARTIFACT, no visual effect: the mock's base font-size is 13px and the live fixture inherits 13.6px (0.85rem). Every text node in this card sets its own size (title 13, meta 9.5, body 11.5, note 9, dling/ok 11.5, watch 10.5), so this is the inherited base only and renders nothing.
expectDivergence.flexGrow: HARNESS ARTIFACT: the mock's `.ol-col .fr-gcard{flex:1}` is CATALOG chrome that equalises the five comparison columns. The live card is not in that grid.

expectDivergence.text: PRD-P8 D5 — the mock's "5.6 GB" is not a real Qwen3-4B GGUF quant size; 4.3 GB is the verified Q8_0 figure (4,280,404,704 B). Frozen: honesty over parity.

expectDivergence.extra: live-only wrapper. The mock lets the card's own flex column space the foot; the live card wraps it in `.fr-gcard__foot` (margin-top:auto) so the two gate cards' CTAs align at unequal body heights, and so the whole foot can be ONE role="status" aria-live region (PRD-P8 §5) — states ①→detected and ③→④ change with no user action and must be announced.

expectDivergence.fontSize: HARNESS ARTIFACT, no visual effect: the mock's base font-size is 13px and the live fixture inherits 13.6px (0.85rem). Every text node in this card sets its own size (title 13, meta 9.5, body 11.5, note 9, dling/ok 11.5, watch 10.5), so this is the inherited base only and renders nothing.

HARNESS ARTIFACT, no visual effect: the mock's base font-size is 13px and the live fixture inherits 13.6px (0.85rem). Every text node in this card sets its own size (title 13, meta 9.5, body 11.5, note 9, dling/ok 11.5, watch 10.5), so this is the inherited base only and renders nothing.
expectDivergence.flexWrap: live-only, deliberate: the shipped card is narrower than the mock's catalog column, so the action row wraps instead of overflowing.

expectDivergence.color: PRE-EXISTING, verified at 5c890515 (before this branch): `.gbtn--pri` uses --color-accent-contrast, commented in onboarding.css as "design literal #0b0a0e". The mock hard-codes the literal; the FTUE tokenized it repo-wide for every primary gate button, not just these states. rgb(11,10,14) vs rgb(8,19,29) — both near-black on the accent fill. Not PRD-P8 drift; changing it would be a separate, surface-wide decision.

expectDivergence.absent: not a visual divergence, a MEASUREMENT one — both dots are unmeasurable here. The live dot is a `::before` pseudo-element (onboarding.css `.fr-dep .watch::before`, 6px, --color-text-subtle), which per-element getComputedStyle cannot address; the mock's `<span class="d">` has NO matching CSS rule in the vendored stylesheet at all and measures 0×0. PRD-P8 §5 specifies a 6px dot for this line, so the live rendering is the intended one.

expectDivergence.fontSize: HARNESS ARTIFACT, no visual effect: the mock's base font-size is 13px and the live fixture inherits 13.6px (0.85rem). Every text node in this card sets its own size (title 13, meta 9.5, body 11.5, note 9, dling/ok 11.5, watch 10.5), so this is the inherited base only and renders nothing.

expectDivergence.color: the mock hard-codes the literal #6aa88f (jade); no design-system token equals it exactly, so the live rule uses --color-success — the same substitution `.ln__check` already makes. Recorded in onboarding.css's PRD-P8 header block. Every OTHER property on this line (size, weight, gap) is scored normally and is NOT covered by this declaration.
expectDivergence.borderColor: Same single decision as this element's `color` — the icon and border inherit `currentColor`. The mock hard-codes #6aa88f (a muted jade) and no design-system token equals it; the live rule uses --color-success, the substitution `.ln__check` already makes.

expectDivergence.borderRadius: visually identical: 50% and 999px both render a circle on a square element.

expectDivergence.fontSize: HARNESS ARTIFACT, no visual effect: the mock's base font-size is 13px and the live fixture inherits 13.6px (0.85rem). Every text node in this card sets its own size (title 13, meta 9.5, body 11.5, note 9, dling/ok 11.5, watch 10.5), so this is the inherited base only and renders nothing.

expectDivergence.width: fixture arithmetic, not a style delta. The mock hard-codes width:42% (42% of a 5.6 GB total that does not exist); the live fixture sets the honest completed/total = 2.4 / 4.3 GB = 56%. The fill's SKIN (height, radius, background, transition) is scored normally.
expectDivergence.fontSize: HARNESS ARTIFACT, no visual effect: the mock's base font-size is 13px and the live fixture inherits 13.6px (0.85rem). Every text node in this card sets its own size (title 13, meta 9.5, body 11.5, note 9, dling/ok 11.5, watch 10.5), so this is the inherited base only and renders nothing.

expectDivergence.text: TWO declared deltas in one line. (a) PRD-P8 D5 — "2.4 / 5.6 GB" → "2.4 / 4.3 GB", the verified Q8_0 total. (b) PRD-P8 D4a-2 — the tail reads "downloading in the background", not the mock's "type your first prompt while it lands": D4 keeps the user ON the gate while an auto-started pull runs, and there is no composer there, so the mock's line would be a lie. The mock's wording IS used verbatim once the user has advanced (composer/ack).

expectDivergence.extra: PRD-P8 D4a-1 — the mock's ③ foot has no action row because the mock has already auto-advanced the user to the composer (~1.4s after detection). D4 deliberately does not steal the stage, so the card stays mounted and the user needs a way forward.

PRD-P8 D4a-1 — the "Continue →" button. It is the MINIMUM affordance that makes D4 navigable (advance without restarting the in-flight pull); the mock has no analog. Hosts wire it through the Gate slot's `ctx.onContinue`, and the card renders no button when it is omitted. NOTE: the live fixture passes `onContinue` itself, so this report measures the CORRECTLY-WIRED card — an unwired host is a host-test concern (FirstRunGate / FirstRunSurfaceMount), never something a parity report can catch.

elements.label: stopped.action
elements.note: live renders this ONLY when the server reports runtime_managed (PRD-P8 §5); the live fixture pins the DESKTOP posture (runtimeManaged: true), which is the only posture the mock draws. On web / containerised self-host the button is correctly absent and the foot degrades to the instructional watch line — a state the mock has no column for.
